# Material and Methods

<!-- For detailed guidance on all R Markdown features shown below, see:
     vignette("uhhthesis-tutorial-en", package = "UHHthesis") -->

The structure of this chapter depends very much on the type of research study, whether it is a field, laboratory or modelling study or a literature review. For field studies, the typical subsections are the

- study site
- experimental set-up
- sampling design
- statistical analysis with information on the used computer program ^[such as [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) - this is an example of a footnote]


## Study site

External images (e.g. maps) are embedded with `knitr::include_graphics()`. The caption is set via `fig.cap`:

```{r location, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Location of sampling site....", out.width = "50%"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/sampling_site.jpg")
```

Note that in Word output, images are always displayed at full width. The `out.width` chunk option does not work for Word. For external images, the width can be adjusted via the image file size itself:

```{r location-small, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Map of the sampling location in reduced size."}
knitr::include_graphics("images/sampling_site_small.jpg")
```


## Cross-references

Figures are referenced with `\@ref(fig:<chunk-label>)`, tables with `\@ref(tab:<chunk-label>)`. Chunk labels must **not** contain underscores — use hyphens instead. Sections can be linked via `[Section name]`, e.g. [Discussion]. For a full overview, see the tutorial.


## Mathematical equations

Inline equations use single dollar signs: $E = mc^2$. Display equations use double dollar signs: $$E = mc^2$$

For numbered equations with cross-references, use the LaTeX equation environment (see eq. \@ref(eq:mean)):

\begin{equation}
  \bar{X} = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n X_i}{n}
  (\#eq:mean)
\end{equation}

Formulas should be integrated into sentences and end with a comma or period.


## Chemical formulas

Use `$\mathrm{...}$` to avoid automatic italicisation in math mode:

- Subscript: $\mathrm{CH_4}$
- Superscript: $\mathrm{O^-}$
- Combined: $\mathrm{Fe_2^{2+}Cr_2O_4}$ via `$\mathrm{Fe_2^{2+}Cr_2O_4}$`
- Hydrate bullet: CuCl $\bullet$ $\mathrm{7H_{2}O}$
- Arrows: $\longrightarrow$ (reaction), $\rightleftharpoons$ (reversible), $\leftrightarrow$ (resonance)
- Delta: $\Delta$

Numbered reaction equations use the equation environment:

\begin{equation}
  \mathrm{C_6H_{12}O_6 + 6O_2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{6CO_2 + 6H_2O}
  (\#eq:reaction)
\end{equation}

Reference via \@ref(eq:reaction). Unnumbered inline reactions use dollar signs: $\mathrm{NH_4Cl_{(s)}} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NH_{3(g)}+HCl_{(g)}}$


## Software

This section should always come at the end of the method chapter. You should list here the R version you used and each package with its version number, including the reference. The following text creates everything necessary automatically. You only need to update the packages used:

All analyses were performed using the statistical software R (version `r paste(R.Version()$major, R.Version()$minor, sep = ".")`) [@R-base]. This thesis, including tables, was generated using the packages 'bookdown' (version `r packageVersion("bookdown")`) [@R-bookdown], 'rmarkdown' (version `r packageVersion("rmarkdown")`) [@R-rmarkdown], 'knitr' (version `r packageVersion("knitr")`) [@R-knitr], and 'flextable' (version `r packageVersion("flextable")`) [@R-flextable].


<!-- If you want the next chapter to start on a new page type (LaTeX works also with Word output): -->
\newpage
